summary: Add explicit M2 runtime save/load support
acceptance_criteria: M2 runtime save/load explicitly preserves the M2 economy, population, agriculture, market, and route/transport slices required by M2-ECON-POP-001 and M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001, or it returns a structured unsupported-save rejection before any M2 host hash or exit claim can use save/load.
Same seed, same M2 fixture, same command sequence, and same saved snapshot produce the same state hash after load as before save in the Node path.
The existing M1 save/load golden and corrupt-save rejection behavior remains compatible and still passes.
Malformed, missing, future-version, or cross-manifest M2 save data is rejected with stable reason codes/paths and does not mutate the current runtime.

Save DTO/schema changes remain explicit, serializable, versioned, and confined to the existing save-format/sim-core boundary; no platform IO, IndexedDB, Electron IPC, compression, database, or production dependency is introduced.
This task resolves M2-ECON-POP-FOLLOW-UP-001 enough that M2-HOST-HASH-CANARY-001 may perform its required save/load hash comparison without overstating final M2 exit performance.
required_tests: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M2-SAVE-RUNTIME-001
notes: This task exists to resolve the M2 runtime save/load follow-up recorded during M2-ECON-POP-001 and kept through M2 pressure validation.

Prefer the smallest explicit v1-compatible extension that preserves M2 state hash determinism. If that requires changing a frozen save-format decision instead of extending the existing explicit DTO/codec contract, stop and route to systems_architect before implementation continues.

Do not use this task to implement platform persistence, IndexedDB, Electron filesystem saves, migrations beyond explicit v1 handling, compression, cloud saves, or gameplay scope beyond persisted M2 slices.
